cases.focus: headers are case-insensitive, requests encode query/body data, and options serialize redirect/timeout policy
response DTO JSON decoding, success status, centralized error extraction, and redirect/client/server exception classification
HttpTransporter converts DTO requests into PSR-7 requests and merges request/parameter options for a fake client
WP_AI_Client_HTTP_Client prepares WordPress HTTP args and converts a fake WordPress HTTP response into PSR-7
WP_AI_Client_HTTP_Client maps a fake WP_Error response into an AI-client NetworkException

invalid_options.message: Request option "timeout" must be greater than or equal to 0.

ai-http:wordpress-adapter-error.message: Network error occurred while sending GET request to https://wp.example/fail: Connection refused

invalid_options.message: Request option "timeout" must be greater than or equal to 0.

ai-http:wordpress-adapter-error.message: Network error occurred while sending GET request to https://wp.example/fail: Connection refused

remaining_gaps.detail: The fixture uses fake PSR-18 and WordPress HTTP transports. Real wp_safe_remote_request network behavior, HTTPlug discovery against environment clients, provider API calls, and TLS/error propagation remain later gates.
The fixture covers the HTTP boundary only. Provider registry, model metadata, text/image generation operations, event dispatch, caching, and tool/function call behavior remain later AI-client gates.
Trackback receipt/ping behavior and privacy request mail/list-table flows are still outside this fixture.
The fixture compares copied oracle PHP in both roots; generated original-path PHP replacement remains a later cross-domain gate.
